title: Prokaryotes vs Eukaryotic Cells
summary: Prokaryotes lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles (e.g. Bacteria, Blue-green algae), while Eukaryotes possess a membrane-enclosed nucleus and complex organelles (e.g. Plant, Animal, Fungi).
points: Prokaryotes have circular naked DNA; Eukaryotes have linear DNA wrapped around Histones.

title: Plant Cell vs Animal Cell
summary: Plant cells have a rigid cellulose cell wall, large central vacuole, and plastids (chloroplasts). Animal cells lack cell walls and plastids but possess centrioles/centrosomes for cell division.
points: Cell Wall present in Plants (Cellulose), Fungi (Chitin), Bacteria (Peptidoglycan).
Centrosome present only in Animal cells.
Dictyosomes are unattached Golgi units in Plant cells.
